name: Apache Software Foundation
reason: canonical open-source federation with explicit community governance and meritocratic contributor model

name: Linux Foundation
reason: largest open-source umbrella foundation; hosts Kubernetes CNCF and 200+ projects under shared governance

name: Mozilla Foundation
reason: 501(c)(3) open-source and internet-health nonprofit; explicitly commons-oriented mission

name: Wikimedia Foundation
reason: fiscal host for 40+ language-edition chapters; open knowledge commons; CC BY-SA license

name: Creative Commons
reason: legal infrastructure for the knowledge commons; directly enables free-culture movement

name: Creative Commons Corporation
reason: legal entity for Creative Commons; same mission

name: Open Source Initiative
reason: steward of OSI-approved licenses; defines open-source governance standards

name: Internet Archive
reason: digital preservation commons; public interest 501(c)(3); open access to human knowledge

name: Electronic Frontier Foundation
reason: civil liberties org defending digital rights; donor-supported nonprofit

name: Software Freedom Conservancy
reason: fiscal host for 50+ open-source projects including Git and Inkscape; explicitly copyleft-oriented

name: NumFOCUS
reason: fiscal sponsor for scientific Python ecosystem (NumPy Pandas Jupyter SciPy); community-governed

name: Tor Project
reason: 501(c)(3) developing privacy-preserving open-source tools; explicitly anti-surveillance mission

name: The Tor Project
reason: same org as Tor Project; both name variants appear in data

name: GNOME Foundation
reason: member-governed foundation supporting the GNOME desktop; copyleft; elected board

name: Free Software Foundation
reason: original free-software nonprofit; copyleft licenses; explicitly anti-proprietary mission

name: Free Software Foundation Europe
reason: FSFE; advocacy and legal support for software freedom in Europe
